purpose: Defines the swarm-worker subagent role for Claude Code. YAML frontmatter declares the agent name, description (single-task swarm worker), one auto-loaded skill (worker-protocol), and one role-specific hook (PostToolUse on Write -> validate-progress-file.sh). The body documents the dual-path convention (code in {project_root}, progress in {tracker_root}/dashboards/{id}/progress/{task_id}.json), the seven fixed stages (reading_context -> planning -> implementing -> testing -> finalizing -> completed/failed), the 8 mandatory progress writes, and the structured RETURN FORMAT (STATUS / SUMMARY / FILES CHANGED / EXPORTS / DIVERGENT ACTIONS / WARNINGS / ERRORS) the master parses on completion.
gotchas: Workers MUST distinguish {project_root} (code) from {tracker_root} (progress) — confusing them is the most common worker failure mode
The 8 mandatory writes are the bare minimum — validate-progress-log-detail.sh PostToolUse hook nags on stage-relative log count thresholds
RETURN FORMAT keys (STATUS:/SUMMARY:/FILES CHANGED:) are parsed by the master via failure-protocol — typos in headers break parsing
patterns: Single-skill subagent: only worker-protocol auto-loads; the dispatched prompt provides the rest
Hook is PostToolUse (after Write), not PreToolUse — validates without blocking
conventions: Stage names are lowercase snake-friendly underscored (`reading_context`)
